item.title: Fakemon SCPs?
item.content:encoded: Not gonna lie, I'm almost tempted to make Voltran and Mistikal into skips now. I'm not sure which GOI would take Cipher's place in creating them, though; the megalomaniacal "we're gonna conquer the world!" aspirations of their creators are quite important.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A copy of modern alchemical text The Progeny of Paracelsus by A.A. Gilford.

guess Paul should've consulted an alchemist on this one. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
horrifying concept of spawning real-life pikachus and the realistic outcome of them existing, but not quite good enough to get through the fact that this is literally just pikachu

-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Never thought I'd see someone make pikachu a skip and have it actually work, I'm bummed I was late to punch seeing this.

item.title: +1 Interesting Concept
item.content:encoded: I am a huge fan of Pokemon, and it is satisfying to see a Pikachu SCP within the SCP roster. Overall, I love this concept, if I hadn't made that clear previously.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's short but it's good.
Especially the tragic Story in the Addendum.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+Condensed Rant
-okay u have fun

I've always paddle-balled the idea of trying to squeak a Pokemon into the SCP universe, but I could never think of a decent method of doing so. I'm honestly a little annoyed you managed to make it happened, because now the idea isn't original anymore!

On the other hand, I don't think I could've brought myself to be so…dark about the origins. Perhaps it's for the best somebody else beat my (lazy) butt to the goal.

A bizarre way to introduce an SCP that I'm sure many people have considered over the years. It works well, and I was honestly surprised that there weren't any references to Detective Pikachu in here. (Or at least anything obvious.)

Oh, and now I can write a tale about this, so I got that going for me…after all, we still had two other "projects" that were "in development".

It also makes you wonder just exactly how a Pokemon would react to a world like the one that the SCP Foundation exists on…

also this isn't meant to be a hostile comment, so i apologize if my wording makes it seem that way

+1 for getting the job done probably the best way possible.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think I would’ve upvoted if not for the sudden application of pathos at the end. It feels like a cheap way to bring life to this SCP.

Novote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
-1

Not sure whether or not this is actually plagiarism, but it’s still just a Pokémon with some added and removed flare. Cool story, but it doesn’t make up for the SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I had some fridge horror from this one when I eventually realized that horse wombs were specified, but said nothing about the rest of the horse being present.

And of course my imagination had to fill in the rest…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I find this to be a silly and sad and horrific thing — a lot to achieve in the space of such a short article. For my taste you put in just the right density of detail regarding the homunculus process, giving a disturbing sense of it without getting too bogged down in specifics.

item.title: First downvoted, then novoted, then upvoted.
item.content:encoded:
I think i read this like 6 times before i came to my final conclusion, which is an upvote.

that sure is pikachu alright.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
damn pikachu OWNED that kid, smh paul

1+

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I actually really love this, of course for the log at the end but also the description of the "Pikachu's" remind me of the stock animations from Hey You, Pikachu! It really calls to mind for me a living creature with a dull, lifeless AI for a brain which I enjoy a lot, and also the tragedy that comes as a consequence of it… +1

item.title: Re: -1, sorry Tan
item.content:encoded: Would more visual elements help? A picture is worth a thousand words. And characters like Pikachu and pop culture in general are very reliant on imagery.

item.title: Re: +1 Simple, compelling, well executed
item.content:encoded:
Overall, I think this article is a good example of the brilliance of a short work executed well and an example of why I like shorter works overall

Agreed. I love the shorter articles that are well-executed: it's so tough to get the pacing right that when you do, it really sells.

I'd love to have another Short Skip contest — think we'll ever do another one?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Containment and Description sections either need to be longer or shorter, I can't decide, but right now they feel like they're both hovering in a weird awkward length.

Thanks for the gut punch at the last entry, though. A clever twist on "sad lonely old man" suicide with great pacing. Very, very well done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's a bit on the nose, but still tells a good story.
no-vote. But might change after a re-read later.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, to be honest I don’t think the discovery log is at all sufficient for a reference played this straight. You’ve got the beginning of something interesting here but merely rattling (hehe) off a few ingredients does not a backstory make. -1 in its present form.

item.title: +1 Simple, compelling, well executed
item.content:encoded:
For these reasons, it is currently unclear whether SCP-4974 is sapient or even possesses a consciousness at all.

This could be shortened to "It is currently unclear if SCP-4974 possesses a consciousness at all."

Overall, I think this article is a good example of the brilliance of a short work executed well and an example of why I like shorter works overall.
you did it you absolute madman

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really wanted to focus on implications in the addendum rather than go into huge detail about the specifics of the homunculus process, but I can understand how that'd be disappointing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm with Peppers on this one, but I really wish more focus had been spent on the actual alchemical process. It's so weird and unique and the story told by the addendum is so good, but the actual SCP portion of this leaves a little to be desired.

item.title: Re: -1, sorry Tan
item.content:encoded: Same for me.

item.title: Re: -1, sorry Tan
item.content:encoded: Yeah, I can understand where you're both coming from here, but I feel like the fact that in-universe this is just the guy trying to create pikachu in real life - along with him then treating the real-life pikachu as if it were still the cartoon - means that's not really something I can change while keeping the same story here. Sorry you didn't care for this one!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The description is so straight forward that I started to doubt whether the addendum could turn me around on this one, but by golly it did. Hoo boy, it did. +1

item.title: Re: -1, sorry Tan
item.content:encoded: This pretty much sums up my thoughts on it too.

item.title: -1, sorry Tan
item.content:encoded: I feel like this article suffers from not enough content to drive the pop culture reference, and though it features a horrifying discovery log, that doesn't take away from the fact that this is literally just pikachu in real life. There's a story there, and it's pretty gruesome and well written, but in my opinion doesn't make up for the lack of creative anomaly.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded: Thanks to Gee0765 and DrAkimoto for their feedback! May God have mercy on my soul.
